_note: Runtime projection state. NOT canon. The claims below are canon; which of them are rivals of which, and how an observation selects between them, is campaign state. See lab/evals/memory-systems-v1/alternatives.md (hash-bound into the rival Claims).
_outcome_map_note: Ordered by precedence. Evaluated top-down, first match selects. Row 1 outranks the rest because falsification of the primary is dispositive regardless of the rivals.

outcome_map.observation: any subject achieves mean recall@1 >= 0.80 at d=10 (95% bootstrap CI lower bound >= 0.80), default config
outcome_map.rationale: The primary claim is directly FALSIFIED by its own pre-registered criterion. Dispositive regardless of what the rivals predict.

outcome_map.observation: all subjects < 0.80 at d=10 AND the no-memory full-context control also scores < 0.80
outcome_map.rationale: A2 (measurement invalidity). Nothing scores 0.80 here, including a naive baseline, so the harness cannot discriminate memory quality. The finding is about the task, not the systems.

outcome_map.observation: all subjects < 0.80 at d=10 AND control >= 0.80 AND at least one subject >= 0.80 under vendor best-practice config
outcome_map.rationale: A3 (configuration). The harness discriminates and the ceiling is reachable — the eval measured defaults, not capability.

outcome_map.observation: all subjects < 0.80 at d=10 AND control >= 0.80 AND at least one subject >= 0.80 at d=4
outcome_map.rationale: A4 (depth). The failure is depth-dependent, so the 10-session choice — not memory capability — is doing the work in the primary claim.

outcome_map.observation: all subjects < 0.80 at d=10 AND control >= 0.80 AND no subject >= 0.80 at d=4 or under best-practice config
outcome_map.rationale: Primary SUPPORTED. The harness can discriminate, the ceiling is not reachable by configuration, and the failure is not a depth artifact. Every rival is excluded.

_verifier_note: lineage is the anti-laundering key: the scheduler will not propose a verifier whose lineage already appears in the evidence set, because re-running a lineage yields a correlated observation, not an independent one. Ranked by tier (bindingness to external reality) descending.

verifier_plan.challenge: Run multi_session_coherence at d=10 against a no-memory full-context control within the same 30k-token budget.
Re-run all subjects configured per each vendor's published best-practice guide.
Run multi_session_coherence at d in {2,4,6,8} for all subjects.
Run the held-out reset_semantics suite once, at publication time.
Send pre-publication results to each vendor and log the rebuttal as Evidence.
Have a third party reproduce the headline result on their own harness.

validity_threats.description: No control condition: four subjects, zero controls. A universal sub-0.80 result cannot distinguish weak memory systems from an undiscriminating task.
validity_threats.mitigation: Rival A2 + verifier no_memory_control. Outcome-map row 2 makes the control arm mandatory to reach any conclusion at all.

validity_threats.description: The task corpus may appear in the subjects' training data.
validity_threats.mitigation: reset_semantics stays held out; corpus items generated from a private seed; contamination check reported with results.

validity_threats.description: Vendor APIs change between runs, so cells collected on different dates are not comparable.
validity_threats.mitigation: Pin and record API/model versions inside every Evidence artifact; run all arms within a single window.

validity_threats.description: Every result derives from one harness, so all evidence shares a failure mode and is not independent.
validity_threats.mitigation: Verifier independent_replication (external_commitment tier). The campaign counts independent lineages, not envelopes.

validity_threats.description: A new frontier model with native long-horizon memory changes the field mid-campaign.
validity_threats.mitigation: Decision expiry plus the invalidation conditions below.

validity_threats.description: The cost model (<=30k in / <=3k out per run) can abort a cell. An aborted cell is not a failed cell — coding it as one would manufacture support for the primary claim out of our own budget ceiling.
validity_threats.mitigation: Aborted cells emit Evidence with polarity 'neutral' and an explicit abort reason, never 'contradicts'.

invalidation_conditions: any subject ships a major memory-architecture change
a new frontier model ships with native long-horizon memory
vendor pricing changes enough to materially move cost_per_1k_queries
